summary: Any character in either half may independently change from a consonant to a vowel and alter whether the two counts match, so a complete linear inspection matches the decision problem's lower bound.

optimality.argument: On a balanced consonant-only baseline, changing any single uninspected position to a vowel makes exactly one half's count larger. A correct worst-case algorithm must therefore inspect every position. The signed-balance scan performs constant work per character and meets this Omega(n) adversarial inspection lower bound with O(n) time.

replacement_checks.evidence: An adversary can place the only count-changing vowel at any input position, including the final position inspected in either half.
One midpoint-aware pass performs one fixed-set membership test and at most one counter update per character.
Authored fixtures cover length two, no-vowel equality, repeated vowels, unequal counts in either direction, midpoint placement, and every required letter case behavior.
